body.title: OOP-first — shared state gets a class; device work inherits the design center
body.statement: When shared state crosses functions, a class wraps it: module-level mutable state (dicts, lists, sets) read or written by two or more functions is a failure unless a class encapsulates it (constants and frozen singletons excepted). Device work is done by classes inheriting BaseDevice/BaseShim — no standalone functions doing device work (see unseenuniversity/rules/inherit-base-class). Device code logs through self.log.*, never print(). Inertia tiers for planned edits are defined in unseenuniversity/rules/safeguards; per-piece executor routing in unseenuniversity/rules/collaboration.
body.why: A single well-known entry point per component makes lifecycle and logging uniform — the framework can iterate all devices without knowing their internals. Loose shared state is the torn-tree failure mode in miniature: two writers, no owner, silent drift.
